title: Ochthocosmus sessiliflorus

This family is part of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the order Lamiales, Ochthocerosaceae is one of the few families that are not closely related to other families. The classification of Ochthocerosaceae is still not well understood and is in need of further research. Its extracts have been studied for potential antimalarial properties, with some research suggesting its efficacy against certain strains of the Plasmodium parasite.
